private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
ReOrbit is a Series A space and aerospace company that has raised €45M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies: ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), Sift ($274M Series B), and Aalyria ($1.3B Series B). The broader reference universe includes five public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight additional private peers.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, given that ReOrbit's revenue figures are not available—a constraint that precludes meaningful public-comp P/S anchoring despite a 53.2x median multiple in the public sector. The four-peer median valuation stands at $400M, with a range spanning $274M (Sift floor) to $1.3B (Aalyria ceiling). This cohort directly matches ReOrbit's stage and sector, providing reasonable comparability for early-stage spacecraft and orbital-services businesses.

The triangulation range of $340M to $460M reflects modest variance around the $400M median. Key uncertainties center on revenue trajectory and capital efficiency relative to peers—factors not captured in static valuation multiples. The single-method foundation also creates limited redundancy; access to ReOrbit's revenue or unit economics would strengthen conviction through additional methodologies.
